growth_records.medium: Lake Rotsee water (natural lake water, oxycline)

up.method: 13C-CH4 SIP + nanoSIMS single-cell, Creno445 probe (13C at% 22.00±4.8, n=17; avg biovolume 73.7±8.4 um^3)
13C-CH4 SIP + nanoSIMS single-cell, Mgamma669 probe (avg biovolume 85±8.3 um^3, n=59; assuming same 13C enrichment as Creno445)
per-cell rate (128.0) upscaled by cell count 9.2E3 cell ml^-1 (Creno445); Mgamma669 upscaling gives 1.73 umol l^-1 d^-1

growth_records.cite: Kirsten Oswald et al. Crenothrix are major methane consumers in stratified lakes. The ISME Journal (2017). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2017.77. [PMC5563964]
growth_records.notes: Environmental single-cell 13C-methane uptake rate (Table 1), not classical gDW-based GEM constraint; units fmol C cell^-1 d^-1 and umol l^-1 d^-1 retained verbatim. Per-cell rate computed by authors: 13C at% -> 13C excess (fmol C per cell) using conversion factor 6.4 fmol C um^-3 (Musat et al. 2008) x avg biovolume, corrected for labeling % and 7 d incubation. Population rate f = per-cell c (128.0) x cell count d (9.2E3 cell ml^-1). In situ CH4 at 7 m ~15 umol l^-1; incubations amended to ~1.8 mmol l^-1 CH4, 50 at% 13C. No mu/doubling time reported. Rotsee data from Aug 2013 campaign.

growth_records.medium: Lake Zug water (natural lake water, 160 m anoxic depth)

up.method: 13C-CH4 SIP + nanoSIMS single-cell, Mgamma669 probe (13C at% 13.27±4.9, n=6; avg biovolume 49.7±20.3 um^3)
per-cell rate (74.2) upscaled by cell count 0.4E3 cell ml^-1

sec.method: bulk 13CO2 production, anoxic 15NO3--supplemented incubation (ca. 10-fold over unamended control)
bulk 13CO2 production, anoxic control incubation with no added electron acceptor

growth_records.cite: Kirsten Oswald et al. Crenothrix are major methane consumers in stratified lakes. The ISME Journal (2017). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2017.77. [PMC5563964]
growth_records.snippet: oxidation rates (2.7 umol l-1 d-1 13CO2 produced in 15NO3-supplemented incubation, a ca. 10-fold increase compared to control incubation without any added electron acceptor (0.234 umol l-1 d-1 13CO2 produced)). Lake Zug (anoxic) Crenothrix
growth_records.notes: Anoxic methane-oxidation record: bulk 13CO2 production rate 2.7 umol l^-1 d^-1 (nitrate-amended) vs 0.234 (control) = ~11.5x ratio, consistent with authors' 'ca. 10-fold'. Single-cell CH4-C uptake 74.2 fmol C cell^-1 d^-1 from Table 1 (nanoSIMS T=2 d). Population uptake 0.03 umol l^-1 d^-1 = per-cell x cell count 0.4E3 cell ml^-1. Demonstrates methane-dependent activity/growth under O2-deficient nitrate-reducing conditions. Units verbatim; not gDW-normalized. Oct 2013 campaign.

growth_records.medium: Lake Zug water (natural lake water, 160 m depth) + low O2

up.method: 13C-CH4 SIP + nanoSIMS single-cell, Mgamma669 probe (13C at% 9.26±1.7, n=19; avg biovolume 32.5±5.5 um^3)
per-cell rate (38.1) upscaled by cell count 1.1E3 cell ml^-1

growth_records.cite: Kirsten Oswald et al. Crenothrix are major methane consumers in stratified lakes. The ISME Journal (2017). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2017.77. [PMC5563964]
growth_records.notes: 'Low O2' incubation (~80 umol l^-1 O2). CH4-C uptake 38.1 fmol C cell^-1 d^-1 (Table 1). Population 0.041 umol l^-1 d^-1 = per-cell x 1.1E3 cell ml^-1. O2 conditions per Methods ('ca. 80 umol l-1'). Units verbatim; not gDW-normalized. Detailed rates cross-referenced to Oswald et al. 2016.

growth_records.medium: Lake Zug water (natural lake water, 160 m depth) + high O2

up.method: 13C-CH4 SIP + nanoSIMS single-cell, Mgamma669 probe (13C at% 8.68±1.9, n=10; avg biovolume 32.5±5.5 um^3)
per-cell rate (35.3) upscaled by cell count 1.1E3 cell ml^-1

growth_records.cite: Kirsten Oswald et al. Crenothrix are major methane consumers in stratified lakes. The ISME Journal (2017). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2017.77. [PMC5563964]
growth_records.notes: 'High O2' incubation (~200 umol l^-1 O2). CH4-C uptake 35.3 fmol C cell^-1 d^-1 (Table 1); comparable to low-O2 (38.1), indicating O2 level did not strongly change per-cell uptake. Population 0.038 umol l^-1 d^-1 = per-cell x 1.1E3 cell ml^-1. In this sample 3 filaments with 13C/12C<0.015 excluded. Units verbatim; not gDW-normalized.
